entry.title: Re: Add Attachment to ouptut in Sales Quotation
entry.content: How can we apply a custom enhancement for this output? Thanks, Mona

entry.title: Re: Clarification on SAP S/4HANA APIs for Customer, Billi...
entry.content:
Hi Kavinkumar,
 
For S/4HANA you should first use the standard released APIs available in the SAP API Business Hub. Direct table access and custom CDS services should only be considered if no suitable released API exists.

entry.title: Re: Given a Customer Account set up as a ship-to partner...
entry.content: Yes, in S/4HANA the assignment should be still there. Open BP 5009876 → Sales Area Data → Partner Functions (role FLCU01) and you should see the referencing sold-to (SP).

entry.title: Re: Add Attachment to ouptut in Sales Quotation
entry.content: I opened an influence request for this issue: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/367971

entry.title: Re: Instance for Data Attribute Recommendation cannot be...
entry.content: Likely not your setup. As far as I know, DAR on trial is not provisioned in all regions anymore.

entry.title: Asset table for extracting data in SAP ECC system for uploading data in S4 hana system
entry.content:
Hi All

what is SAP ECC for extracting data to upload in s4 Hana system 

like we have BSIS, BSIK and BSID then what is the table for Assets?

Regards

Jithendra KS

entry.title: Re: How to add stock into Third Party Warehouse
entry.content: @castin you can use thise API for this purpose. https://api.sap.com/api/API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV/overview https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3f57e7df4a114edabffe8b2d581a59ed/d4c919581bc30a02e10000000a44147b.html

entry.title: Re: No Option to choose Item Category "U" - Stock Transfe...
entry.content:
Good day @saijalashok 

Thank you for your question.

That is because you are using Public Cloud. The My Purchase Requisitions app does not provide item category U or L for PR creation in public cloud. See KBA 3040361 and KBA 2564079 for more details.

If your goal is Stock Transfer, I suggest you crate a Stock Transport Order with the purchase order apps, instead of trying to crate a PR with item category U.

If Subcontracting is your aim, then create a Subcontracting Purchase Order directly with the item category L at the level or your Purchase Order.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: How to switch off E-Mail notifcation for flexible wor...
entry.content: In test systems disable e-mail sending via SCOT (set the node inactive or route to a dummy SMTP/mail address).

entry.title: BOM Should Not Create For Boughtout Material Currently no restriction in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4 HANA system allow to create BOM for Boughtout Material but we do not want to create BOM form Boughtout Material.

How can we restrict the same in SAP S/4 hana Public cloud.

Regards,

Ashish

entry.title: Re: Planned expense for the expense which is being consis...
entry.content: Not directly possible as far as I know. If you need YPC1, you can try to create a separate planning version/level and assign that costing variant there (or use a custom valuation).

entry.title: Where to enter data in CO01 so it appears in KKO0?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I would like some guidance on how to make additional information entered during Production Order creation (CO01) appear in the Order Information System report (KKO0) for accounting team?
- Which standard header fields are included in KKO0 without adjusting the report layout?
- Is the “Additional Data” tab the best place to enter information that flows to AUFK/AFKO and shows in KKO0?

Thank you for your guidance.

entry.title: Re: Embed React Components to SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi ,

I am trying to implement stacked column chart with dynamic data using third party scripts inside inline frame. My code is 

<!doctype html>
<html>
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8" />
    <meta
      name="viewport"
      content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1,maximum-scale=1,user-scalable=no"
    />
    <title>Stacked Column (ECharts)</title>
    <style>
      html, body, #chart { height: 100%; width: 100%; margin: 0; }
      body { background: #ffffff; }
    </style>
    <!-- Apache ECharts from jsDelivr -->
    <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/echarts@5/dist/echarts.min.js"></script>
  </head>
  <body>
    <div id="chart" role="img" aria-label="Stacked column chart"></div>
    <script>
      // Parse JSON passed via URL hash
      function parseHash() {
        try {
          const raw = decodeURIComponent(location.hash.slice(1));
          return raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : null;
        } catch (e) {
          console.error("Invalid hash JSON:", e);
          return null;
        }
      }

 

      // Default demo data if none supplied
      const fallback = {
        categories: ["Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri", "Sat", "Sun"],
        series: [
          { name: "Open", data: [120, 132, 101, 134, 90, 230, 210] },
          { name: "In Progress", data: [220, 182, 191, 234, 290, 330, 310] },
          { name: "Closed", data: [150, 232, 201, 154, 190, 330, 410] }
        ],
        yLabel: "Count",
        title: "Work Orders by Day (Stacked)"
      };

 

      const incoming = parseHash() || fallback;

 

      const chart = echarts.init(document.getElementById("chart"));

 

      function render(data) {
        const option = {
          title: data.title ? { text: data.title, left: "center" } : undefined,
          tooltip: { trigger: "axis", axisPointer: { type: "shadow" } },
          legend: { top: 28 },
          grid: { left: 40, right: 20, top: 60, bottom: 40, containLabel: true },
          xAxis: { type: "category", data: data.categories || [] },
          yAxis: { type: "value", name: data.yLabel || "" },
          series: (data.series || []).map(s => ({
            name: s.name,
            type: "bar",
            stack: "total",
            emphasis: { focus: "series" },
            data: s.data || [],
            // Optional: rounded bars
            itemStyle: { borderRadius: [3, 3, 0, 0] }
          }))
        };
        chart.setOption(option, true);
      }

 

      render(incoming);

 

      // Re-render if hash data changes
      window.addEventListener("hashchange", () => {
        const d = parseHash();
        if (d) render(d);
      });

 

      // Handle resize
      window.addEventListener("resize", () => chart.resize());
    </script>
  </body>
</html>
And enabled allow-modals allow-scripts allow-same-origin in sandbox in properties. But still I am getting error as below

Thanks and Regards 

Saranya

entry.title: Re: Account code which is used in foreign currency valuat...
entry.content: That should be possible via configuration. The accounts used by FAGL_FCV are controlled in Prepare Automatic Postings for Foreign Currency Valuation (OB09). Assignment is by valuation area + account determination key, so not directly changeable elsewhere.

entry.title: Re: Value differences are posted solely to stock account...
entry.content: That is standard behavior. MIRO checks each line item individually, so differences post to the stock account. Proportional distribution is not supported as far as I know, so only possible via custom enhancement/BAdI.

entry.title: Error ' Essential transfer parameters are missing in record: "
entry.content:
Hi Team,

While performing the Staging process for a production order, I am using "stage material for production order" app. However, I am encountering the following error.(SAP Public cloud)

Could you please help me understand the cause of this issue and guide me on how to resolve it?

Thank you.
Kiran

entry.title: Field combination not visible when creating new line in business volume criteria
entry.content:
Hi Experts @Lakshmipathi  @GauthierVictor 

Please find the attached screensthots below.

I'm unable to see field combination 0006- customer hierarchy when i'm trying to create new line in the business volume selection criteria in condition contract, i have checked the set field combination and field combination for business volume selection and also specify settlement settings for condition contract types, this combination is present and this field combination is assigned contract type as well, but i'm not able to see this 0006 in standard ones 0S01/0S02 condition contract types and remaining few combinations are visible.

Request you to suggest the missing configuration please.

Settlement Management SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: Bank swift key update file setup automation
entry.content: This is not a standard program and typically needs to be done via a file interface + background job.

entry.title: How to download Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation result or Output Spools in bulk ?
entry.content:
Dear SAP 

When SAP Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation job is run, it generates lots of reports as spool , we need the spool to be downloaded as is required by auditor for FX calculation verification which spool has the data but after the run it generates 100s of output report which is impossible to download every month.

Request you support for way to get the spool data over email or downloaded in bulk in one single archive or a SAP report that provides the same information.

Regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to download Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation r...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

For your requirement of download large amount of data from Result list of Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation, please check out the app Analyze Closing Run Results ( App ID: F3753 ).

With this app, you can display, filter, and analyze the results of Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation jobs in detail. You can also use it for analysis, reporting, and reconciliation purposes. Crucially, it can be used to download all entries from the result list, or only those that you've filtered, into a spreadsheet for further analysis.
Please keep in mind that there is also a limit in Excel line item, so if your Result list contain more than 1 million line item, please consider divide it by filter condition and download them in smaller batch.
Regard.

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Vienna 2026 - April 24th 2026
entry.content:
Meet your peers in beautiful Vienna! 
Exchange knowledge and make the most of the networking opportunities: https://sit.wien/ 

The call for speakers will be closing soon: Call For Speakers | sit.wien

Sponsors are warmly welcome: Become A Sponsor | sit.wien

entry.title: Re: Facing the issue while trying to do the full load for...
entry.content: The message means the package was already extracted once, so a new full load is prevented. You can try to reset/re-initialize the datasource/ODP subscription (ODQMON) and then run the full load again.

entry.title: Business Partner Role Keys are not seen in app F3163 as Manage Business Partner Master Data
entry.content:
Hello

Business Partner Role Keys are not seen in APP F3163

 

This is also active too

Thanks,

i want to understand finally what are all the passible instances types, and what each instances include.
thank you 🙂

entry.title: Re: Unable to access my certification attempt – no response from support and no case visibility
entry.content: Hi! My issue is that I cant even get passed the "Mange session"-moment 22. Every time I either try to enroll for e live session or register to take a exam it asks me to manage my subscrpition

entry.title: Cross Delivery HUs in ASR Scenario with TM
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

It is announced that cross delivery handling units are supported in ASR scenario with the SAP S/4HANA 2025 in SAP. (KBAs: https://me.sap.com/notes/3702034 and https://me.sap.com/notes/3423370) 

Is it only supported with ASR with EWM? or is it also supported with ASR with LWM (lean warehouse management, MM or IM warehouses)? Because I think this part is not clear.

And for both ASR in EWM and LWM, can unified package building (UPB and TM PB) be used to create cross delivery handling units or is it only be created manually? For me this question is matters a lot, because in the case of TU based integration, there are TU documents that consolidates deliveries, which could be considered as consolidation document to create cross delivery HUs. But in ASR such a consolidation document does not exist, if system is not checking FOs. (BTW ASR is not supporting consolidation package building aka late package building in TM.)

Thanks in advance,

Kaya

entry.title: Re: How to merge several companion contents recorded with...
entry.content: thank you, Kurt, for taking the time to answer. Indeed it is possible to later update the version that was automatically recorded by Companion. Kristina Kunad suggested to replace the context version by the generic 'latest' so the content becomes version independent. That answers the second question I had, and copilot proposed to use producer and manually merge the 'slides' as if my two contents were demos. I'll give both tips a try. Thanks and enjoy the day,

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere - While Selective Deletion in table and Restricted Variable in Analytics Model
entry.content: Is your Column_1 from type integer or string. String doesn't know <=, >= only like or =

entry.title: Perform Maintenance Jobs App
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are using S4 Hana Private Cloud 2023 & Fiori App . Attachment section of perform maintenance jobs are showing multiple object level to attach document . Our requirement is to default only Order object with document type as EAG only . Can any one please suggest how can we achieve this through standard SAP configuration ?

entry.title: Re: How to create a Fiori Elements App for a RAP BO with Transport Selection
entry.content: @friendlycoder Regarding Fiori Elements I only provide documentation links. Try to set the annotation in the CDS view, see Sample Code ABAP CDS Annotation: https://ui5.sap.com/#/topic/c4e45a37f62f4ed3953918650515ed2c

entry.title: Using SAP Enable Now as a platform for mandatory training
entry.content:
Hello SAP Enable Now experts, we are currently evaluating our future learning and enablement architecture and we would like to understand whether SAP Enable Now can be used not only as a content creation tool, but also as a platform for delivering and managing mandatory training. Our goal is to use one solution for creating, assigning and tracking e-learning courses (for example compliance, onboarding or role-based mandatory training).

We would appreciate your guidance on the following topics.

1. SAP Enable Now as a learning delivery platform

Is it possible to use SAP Enable Now Manager as a learning platform where:

  • learners access assigned courses

  • training is role-based

  • learning content is structured into curricula or learning paths

If yes:

  • what needs to be configured?

  • what are the technical and functional prerequisites?

  • are there any limitations compared to a standard LMS?

2. Assignment of mandatory training

We need to assign mandatory courses to specific users or roles.

Is it possible in SAP Enable Now to:

  • assign training to users or user groups?

  • define that a course is mandatory?

  • set a due date for completion?

How is this managed from an administrator perspective?

3. Tracking and reporting

For audit and compliance purposes we need to track:

  • who has completed the training

  • who has not completed the training

  • completion date

  • learning progress

  • test results / pass-fail status

Can SAP Enable Now Manager provide this level of reporting?

Are there standard reports available for this scenario?

4. Notifications and reminders

Is it possible to automatically send:

  • training assignment notifications

  • reminders for incomplete training

  • escalation for overdue courses?

If yes:

  • is this handled directly in SAP Enable Now

  • or via integration with another SAP solution (e.g. SAP BTP, IAS, LMS)?

5. Recurring and re-certification training

Many mandatory trainings must be repeated regularly.

Can SAP Enable Now:

  • automatically reassign training after a defined period?

  • support recurring certification scenarios?

6. Identity management and authorizations

What is the recommended setup for:

  • user provisioning

  • role-based content assignment

  • SSO integration

in this learning scenario?

7. Licensing model

We would like to understand the licensing impact if SAP Enable Now is used as a learning delivery platform for a large number of learners.

Specifically:

  • which license type is required for learners consuming the training?

  • is there any difference if the content is accessed via Manager?

  • how should we size the licenses for this scenario?

8. Recommended architecture and best practices

From SAP’s perspective:

  • Is this a recommended scenario?

  • In which cases should SAP Enable Now be combined with an LMS?

  • Are there reference architectures or best practices available?

9. Available documentation and enablement materials

Could you please share links to:

  • official documentation

  • configuration guides

  • learning architecture recommendations

  • real customer use cases

for this scenario?

Thank you very much for your support.
Your recommendations will help us define the target enablement and learning architecture.

Kind regards,

Helena Černá

entry.title: Re: IaC for SAP BTP: Automating Environment Management with Terraform and CI/CD
entry.content:
@Atul22Joshi For CF you can use the Terraform provider for CF (https://registry.terraform.io/providers/cloudfoundry/cloudfoundry/latest) that is maintained by the same team as the Terraform provider fro SAP BTP. This is working well.

For Kyma or Kubernetes there is the generic Kubernetes provider (https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/kubernetes/latest). It depends if you want to go for Terraform to manage Kubernetes rersources or if you want to stick to a K8s-native tooling like helm.

entry.title: Nota Fiscal Debit Note processing requirement in case of purchase returns (Partial Refusal)
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Considering Brazil tax reforms and to follow mandatory compliance legislation, we have business requirement to issue a nota fiscal debit note in case there is supplier returns scenario with partial quantity rejection/ refusal by the recipient. At present (currently) the NFE is getting issued manually by the recipient with reference to original NFE to register.

With the mandatory compliance legislation for tax reforms in Brazil, in future the recipient (Ordering Organization) would like to issue a nota fiscal debit note in place of NFE (current process). Please help me to know if SAP has released any Brazil specific standard correction/ KBA notes that can help to address this business requirement. Appreciate your response. Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: End-to-End Guide for Process Management in SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation
entry.content:
Hi Michael, 

thanks for a detailed blog about the process management in SAP Cloud ALM.

I have one use case that moves the Solution Documentation from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM and the test cases and test documents are attached at the process as well as at process steps level. 

I could see the assignment of the document to the the process step when I click on the solution acitivity from the Solution process flow diagram. However I could not see the relation of the process step (solution actitvity) towards the test case. When I go to the Solution Activity App and choose one, I then could see the test case assignment to the selected solution actitvity. Will it be planned to show the relation from solution actitvity to test case when navigating from thr process flow diagram in the future? Could you give me some insights about that? Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Closing Month in SAC (BW Import Model)
entry.content:
@onursoyer 

You could explore creating a Date Input control with range option and then selecting custom Date. This will create a date picker which you can configure to month granularity. This can act as selection for the close period. Then you configure your widgets with current selection and then for the other trend chart with an offset of -12 or -13 .

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: Customizing Remote Function Adapter's Component List for SAP PAPM On Premise
entry.content:
Profitability and Performance Management (PAPM) offers a list of Remote Function Adapter (RFA) types which can be used to communicate to other applications and systems. This is done by reading or by posting data, on the same system as PAPM, or on remote systems (for example, SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA). 

Each RFA type comes with a predefined list of fields, show as components on the function's Rules Tab. To these components, the user can map fields which provide input values or store output results. The RFA works as a wrapper, by calling SAP BAPIs, custom function modules or OData services, and mapping its list of components to the corresponding importing and exporting parameters. 

Until now, to extend a function's list of components in order to meet the specific needs of a user, the user would create a new feature request here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3041.

To address the users' demands for flexibility and efficiency, I will guide you on how to customise the list of RFA components, and add new fields to it. 

1. Identifying the SAP function module called by the RFA function. 

Use the table below to find which BAPI is called by your RFA type. 

RFA typeTechnical abbreviationSAP Function ModuleNote
Finance Accounts PayableFI_APBAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST 
Finance Accounts Payable ItemsFI_APIBAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST 
Finance Accounts ReceivableFI_ARBAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST 
Finance Accounts Receivable ItemsFI_ARIBAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST 
Finance ExtendedFI_EXTBAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST 
Finance General LedgerFI_GLBAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST 
Finance General Ledger ItemsFI_GLIBAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST 
Purchase OrderPOBAPI_PO_CREATE1Limitation: systems with version of S/4 HANA OP1610 or lower - see details below
Primary Cost RepostingPRBAPI_ACC_PRIMARY_COSTS_POSTLimitation: systems with version of S/4 HANA OP1610 or lower - see details below

Sales and Distribution

type = 'SALESORDER'

type = 'QUOTATION'

type = 'INQUIRY'

SD

 

BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2

BAPI_QUOTATION_CREATEFROMDATA2

BAPI_INQUIRY_CREATEFROMDATA2

Limitation: systems with version of S/4 HANA OP1610 or lower - see details below
Secondary Cost Element PostingSPBAPI_ACC_MANUAL_ALLOC_POST 

 

2. Identifying the function module parameter and field you need

Let's have a simple example, where the user wants to add the field 'Company ID of Trading Partner' to RFA FI_AP. 

  • On your target system, run transaction SE37.
  • Fill in function module name - BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST and click the Display button (Figure 1). 
  • Go to tab called Tables (Figure 2).
  • Double click on the associated type BAPIACGL09, corresponding to parameter name ACCOUNTGL.
  • Identify the field TRADE_ID, which has the type CHAR 6, decimals 0, and the description 'Company ID of Trading Partner' (Figure 3). Copy these field technical details.

 Figure 1

Figure 2

Figure 3

3. Maintaining the RFA customizing view

Before modifying the customizing view, we need to check that the necessary placeholder for our field already exists. This is a mandatory condition for the end user to independently add their desired fields.

A. Identifying the placeholder

  • On your local system, run transaction /N/NXI/P1_MF.
  • Navigate in the tree to 'Remote Function Placeholder'.
  • In the list, search for the Placeholder which has the same exact name as the BAPI parameter you just viewed. For our current example, this is 'ACCOUNTGL' (Figure 4).

 Figure 4

Once we make sure that we have all the necessary information, we can move forward to the final step.

B. Adding the new RFA component.

  • Navigate in the tree to 'Remote Function Adapter Mapping'. 
  • Fill in Remote Function Adapter Type with the value 'FI_AP' (code for Finance Accounts Payable), then press 'Enter'.
  • Press the 'Change' button (or Ctrl+F1) and then the 'New Entries' button (or F5).
  • Add all the necessary technical information for the field. (Figure 5)
    Component: structure_name-field_name. Each field belongs to a group of fields, which are then mapped to a BAPI structure. It helps to follow the examples of the fields already defined.
    Sequence: the order in which the components appear on the application UI, on the RFA Rules tab.
    Placeholder: must have the same technical name as the BAPI's parameter.
    Is Mandatory: when choosing Yes, the component will be colored on the application UI. It will red, when the field is not mapped to a value or an environment field, and green, once the field is mapped.
    Declaration Type: RFA components which will be passed to processing are of type Input. RFA components which represent results of processing are of type Output. 
    DDIC Data Type, Data Length, Data Decimals and Name are all technical information taken from the BAPI field definition. 
  • Press 'Save' and select an appropriate Transport Request.

 Figure 5

After activating the objects and releasing the transport requests, the newly added field can be found on the application UI.

❗Limitations

Please note this user-led customization process has the following limitations:

  1. Only available from some RFAs (listed in the table above), where the functionality is simple enough that it can easily align with the customization. 
  2. Only available for systems with version of S/4 HANA OP1610 or lower, in case of RFA PO, PR, SD. PAPM is optimized for S/4HANA 1610. If S/4HANA Version is higher than 1610, the parameters of SAP function modules may have changed data types. This would require more complex work in order to add new RFA components, not just performing the above steps. 
  3. Only available for fields of BAPI parameters which can already be found in the Placeholders list. Adding a new placeholder would require more complex work, including source code changes.

If you find that you are facing one of the above limitations, please create a new feature request for the enhancement you wish to acquire, here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3041.

 

By following these steps - identifying the SAP function module name, identifying the parameter name and field technical details, maintaining the RFA's customizing view, you now have everything you need to complete the process from start to finish. Once you implement this guide, you'll save time, gain agency and get better and faster results every time you repeat the process. Start with a field and carefully test the results of your work. 

If you run into questions, drop them in the comments and I am happy to help!

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Transport only Import/Upload-Jo...
entry.content: Okay. So that means if I add a Import/Upload-Job in the Dev Tenant and want to transport it into the Prod Tenant I need to use the "Don't overwrite objects or data" Option to only transport the new Job. Correct?

entry.title: Cloud ALM Health Monitoring for S/4HANA SAPConnect
entry.content:
Hi support,

Regarding Cloud ALM Health Monitoring for S/4HANA SAPConnect, we see metrics SAPConnect Send Errors (Today) which is attached in this case, but can't find the monitoring tile.

So the monitoring tile for SAPconnect exist or not?


--- Support Assistant ---
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: How to Use


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALM Health Monitoring for S/4HANA SAPConnect
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Greetings from SAP Support.

The metric "SAPConnect Send Errors (Today)" is a newly released metric. To enable data collection for this metric, a minimum version of ST-PI 740 SP33 is required on the managed system.

Please check if your SAP S/4HANA managed system meets the prerequisite.

Once your SAP S/4HANA managed system has been upgraded to ST-PI 740 SP33, the metric will become available and visible in the monitoring tile accordingly.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: lease contract & ROU
entry.content: Dear Expert, Thank you for your previous response. Regarding the 【Configuration Required】 , I have completed the first three items. 4. Assign Valuation Area to Accounting Principle — Could you please advise where this configuration is located? 5. Maintain multiple valuation views in lease contract — How should I go about maintaining multiple valuation views? I noticed that in the contract maintenance interface, I can maintain multiple valuation parameters for a contract. May I kindly ask if this is the correct place to maintain them? Thank you in advance for your guidance. Best regards！

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere - While Selective Deletion in table and Restricted Variable in Analytics Model
entry.content: Ohk rookie mistake.. 🙂🤐😓

entry.title: Re: BOM Should Not Create For Boughtout Material Currentl...
entry.content:
The most robust approach in Public Cloud is to use Custom Logic (Key User Extensibility):

  • Add a validation on the BOM creation app (Maintain Bill of Material).
  • If the material type = bought-out, raise an error message and stop the process.

This way, you don’t rely solely on user discipline but enforce system-level control.

entry.title: editing DMS document with retention period in Opentext
entry.content:
Hello Good Morning,

I would greatly appreciate your help with this question. I had a collection in OpenText with a configured retention period. The client wanted me to remove this retention period because they want to edit DMS documents after they have been archived (after check-in). After removing the retention period from the collection, new documents created and archived can be edited without issue. However, older documents created when the collection had a retention period cannot be edited. Can you explain if this is normal? Is there any way to fix this so that the older documents can also be edited?

entry.title: Re: Can we save the Sales & Delivery doc print and share...
entry.content: If your query is answered, please close the discussion by clicking "Accept as Solution"; else, update here what issue still you are facing

entry.title: Re: EarlyWatch Alert アーカイブ
entry.content: ご回答ありがとうございます。レポートRDSMOPREDUCEDATAを利用して、データベース内のセッション情報を削除しましたが、テーブルDSVASRESULTSGENの容量に変化が見られませんでした。テーブルの内容はDBACOCKPITから確認しています。データスペースを解放する方法についてご教授ください。

entry.title: Re: Transport Request from SAP ECC to S4HANA Sandbox

entry.title: Re: Is it supported to map a master‑data custom field int...
entry.content: Dear Nakao-san, In the SSCUI 102569 - Create Payment Medium Formats, I don't think there is an option to actually "include custom reference fields". Instead, you have to make sure the event 06 is configured under the menu "Event Modules for Payment Medium Formats" in the SSCUI 102569. And then, follow the instruction described in [https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/custom-logic-for-payment-files/ba-p/12828846] to assign the value from the created custom field to the ZREFXX field.

entry.title: Re: How to convert Login Method from PWD to SSO automatically for New Users hired from Onboarding [P
entry.content:
Hi Ajeesh,

Thank you for the solution.
Currently, I am encountering an issue with ONB 2.0. External onboardees are not being converted from PWD to SSO as the login method after they are hired in EC via MPH, even though the User Type is being updated from External to Employee in IAS.
However, in the case of direct hires through Add New Employee or Import, the login method is correctly set to SSO according to the solution provided above.
Could you please advise on this issue?

Regards,

Kasidit S.

entry.title: Re: Joule A2A: Connect Code Based Agents into Joule
entry.content:
Hi @felixbartler , thanks for sharing this example. Was trying to deploy and noticed that there are various mistakes in the source code with several spots that miss the '_' sign. Not sure if the dependency you used in your requirements.txt would fix them, but at least a recent sap-ai-sdk-gen will not accept this code 🙂 Got it to work eventually but wanted to point this out as others may run into similar issues.

BR,

Frank

entry.title: Re: Settlement Process for Lump Sum and Slotting Fee usin...
entry.content:
Best Practice Process Design

  • Centralize all trade terms under a structured framework (Condition Contract Management or Settlement Management).
  • Accruals at the time of sale: Each fee/discount should be accrued as a statistical condition in billing or sales order, ensuring visibility in margin analysis.
  • Subsequent settlement: Actual payout/credit memo should be triggered via settlement runs or manual credit memo requests.
  • Transparency in reporting: Link conditions to COPA value fields for Gross-to-Net revenue analysis.

Recommended SAP Solution Approach

1. Condition Contract Management (CCM) – Standard Approach

Use Customer Condition Contracts for rebates, listing fees, shelf rentals, and promotions. Each arrangement (account opening, listing, shelf rental, etc.) can be modeled as a condition type within CCM. Accruals are posted automatically, and settlement generates credit memo requests → credit memos.

2. Settlement Management Framework

Settlement Management in S/4HANA is designed for subsequent settlement of rebates (both customer and vendor).

  • It supports:
    • Condition contracts
    • Business volume selection criteria
    • Automated accrual postings
    • Settlement documents (credit memo requests)

3. Custom Enhancements (if needed)
For Account Opening Fee (one-time, not volume-based), you may need a custom condition type or a Fiori app extension to trigger a credit memo manually. For Other Fees (renovation, flyer, e-commerce), if they are not linked to sales volume, they can be handled as manual settlement conditions or via custom contract types.

entry.title: Re: How to send email with attachments through SAP BTP Jo...
entry.content: Yes, we are using CAP service to send out a email with attachments, but getting error if we attach more than 1 MB files. [remote] - Error: Error during request to remote service: Request failed with status code 502. We are using SendGrid mail service, please suggest.

entry.title: Re: Field combination not visible when creating new line...
entry.content:
Hi @Lakshmipathi, I have rechecked and found that i have missed checking the config Usage control, i have corrected it and its working fine now.  Thank you.

Now, i have put customer hierarchy in condition contract and created 0SG1 -CCM document. Customer hierarchy has three levels, i have maintained first level in CCM doc in field combination 0006. The business volume is captured for three levels. When i'm creating partial settlements and final settlement document system is not determining RES2 value in subsequent partial or final settlement documents but getting determined in delta accrual documents. Is it because the customer hierarchy has 3 levels and system is unable to reverse accruals. Do we need to do any enhancement for this or do we have any config for this, Kindly suggest please. @Lakshmipathi  @GauthierVictor

Can someone refer me to a document, which can make me achieve the object.

People can use a simple example to explain the process.

entry.title: Re: abapgit-agent: AI Coding Assistants for ABAP

entry.title: From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This February
entry.content:
In the age of AI, the skills we build today will define how we work tomorrow. That’s why we’ve launched a monthly blog series that spotlights the latest AI learning assets and upcoming learning opportunities.

Building on my first blog, where I introduced you to our AI live sessions Beyond Hype (which is all about getting productive with SAP Business AI), I wanted to address some of your most asked questions:

  1. What is the difference between Joule skills and Joule agents?

    Joule skills are designed for executing single, predefined tasks independently. These skills enhance SAP's default capabilities by leveraging unstructured inputs to perform specific, context-aware actions, such as data retrieval or system transactions, in a deterministic, predictable manner. Joule Skills integrates with both SAP and third-party APIs to automate and streamline operations such as ticket creation and workflow execution, all initiated through conversational interactions.

    Joule Agents are AI applications that assist business users with decision support and task automation. They use LLMs and reasoning models to determine actions and their sequence, addressing various situations autonomously. Joule Agents can autonomously execute complex multi-step business processes. They engage users to review and approve significant actions that impact the business, such as extending discounts to dissatisfied customers. User requests or specific business events, such as the creation of a new sales order or customer service ticket, can trigger Joule Agents.

    Deep dive by watching this video: What are Joule agents and skills?  

  2. How can we start from scratch with Joule Studio to create our own portfolio of Joule skills and Joule agents?

    To use Joule Studio, the SAP Build developer license (SKU 8019311) is required, and you need to install the SAP Build Process Automation service to leverage the Cloud Integration Automation service scenario for SAP Build. Please note that you need to be on the new unified SAP Build commercial model.

    And of course, you need Joule Base or Premium. Please note, Joule Studio works with any Joule brownfield license, except Joule for Consultants and Joule for Developers. To run custom Joule Skills in your production environments, you do not need AI Units. However, execution of custom Joule Agents will consume AI Units.

    To all Partners, a TDD license is available on the SAP Partner Portal! Read on to learn how to set up Joule Studio for all partner flows and entitlement levels. I am excited to share the link to the free 30-day basic trial. Request now and select workbook: Create a Joule Agent with Joule Studio!

    And lastly, here are the instructions to set up Joule Studio for all customer flows and entitlement levels.

  3. Besides building low-code and no-code agents with Joule Studio, can I also code them in Python?

    Of course, you can! Have a look at the SAP Community Blog Post by @noravonthenen  from the Developer Advocates. In her article, she shares her experience building an AI Agent with LiteLLM, CrewAI, and SAP-RPT-1, and what actually worked. You can also check out this GitHub repository and learn how to Build Your Own AI Agent-Based Solution using the SAP Cloud SDK for AI and the Generative AI Hub in AI Foundation.

However, let’s continue to focus on Joule Studio. In this three-part tutorial series, we will walk you through the complete journey of building your own custom AI agent in Joule Studio, from defining the business use case to extending its capabilities and finally bringing it to life.

Each part builds on the previous one. By the end, you won’t just understand the theory, you’ll have created a fully functional agent tailored to your scenario.

  1.  Define the use case: We start by grounding everything in a concrete scenario for Intelligent Maintenance Order Fulfillment. What problem are we solving? What outcome are we aiming for? This foundation ensures the agent is built with purpose.

     

  2. Extend the Joule Skill: Next, we move from concept to capability. You’ll learn how to extend an existing skill to support the custom requirements of your use case.

     

  3. Create the Joule Agent: Finally, we assemble everything into a working agent — connecting the extended skill and configuring the behavior so it can operate autonomously.

     

Want to dive deeper into the content? Rewatch the session and access the session material via SAP Learning Hub (subscription required) and explore your key resources to help you get started building your own Joule agents:

  • Step-by-step guidance to build custom Joule agents in Joule Studio (recording)
  • Joule Studio Goes Live: Answers to Your Top Questions covering Skills and the new Agent Builder features 
  • Best Practices for Building Agents: Key principles and practices for designing effective Joule Agents 
  • Discovery Center Mission: Get Hands-on experience in creating a Joule Agent with multiple skills and MCP
  • Since you also asked about a certification for Joule Studio, the SAP Build Developer Certification includes Joule Studio.

Hi Joule, where should I get started with the latest learning resources? *

Apart from live enablement sessions, get hands-on with our latest on-demand learning resources:

  • Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core, and Generative AI Hub.
  • Explore how to make Large Language Models truly enterprise-ready by grounding them with your own business data using Document Grounding in SAP Generative AI Hub.
  • Create your own schemas from scratch in the SAP Document AI workspace. Go ahead and try it yourself! 
  • Learn how to deploy an LLM on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad, the orchestration service, and the Python SDK.
  • Get started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python learn how the Machine Learning process develops using a Python Client for SAP HANA.
  • Get started with AI (Vector Engine, NLP, Knowledge Graph[soon]) using SAP HANA and Python, and learn how SAP HANA Cloud supports the development of generative AI applications.
  • Implement enterprise-grade AI solutions using SAP's AI services and integration with generative AI models.

 As you can see, some SAP Samples were used for an SAP CodeJam. An SAP CodeJam is a free, hands-on developer workshop organized by SAP that focuses on a specific technology topic. It’s designed for developers who want practical, guided experience — not just slides, but real coding. If you want to experience a SAP CodeJam in person, check out the upcoming events in your region and sign up!

* Powered by the machine learning technology of SAP AI Business Services, personalized recommendations are generated for employees, in one place on the Learning home page, based on employee data.

As we look ahead, we’re excited to share with you a new lineup of learning opportunities designed to help you expand your knowledge and make AI an advantage in your role:

  • March 5, 11 am CET: Accelerate your transformation to the Cloud with SAP Business AI. Discover how SAP’s AI-powered migration toolchain enables customers to accelerate their transformation with less risk and cost to the SAP Business Suite.
  • March 12, 11 am CET: Efficient Document Inbound Processing in SAP Cloud ERP with SAP Document AI. Explore the pre-delivered document inbound processing use cases for SAP Cloud ERP across Sales, Finance, and Quality Management.
  • March 19, 10 am CET: All Things Business AI: Latest Insights, Updates & Live Demos. Discover current AI trends, recent updates across SAP’s AI portfolio, and see live demos showcasing how Business AI can drive efficiency and innovation in real-world business processes.

Stay tuned for more, and happy learning! 

Further Resources:

  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This October
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This November
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This December
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This January

Joule Studio SAP Business AI Joule  SAP Document AI AI Foundation #sapclouderp #handson

entry.title: Re: abapgit-agent: AI Coding Assistants for ABAP
entry.content: There was a typo in the url. Now it should be working.

entry.title: はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - 計算の追加
entry.content:
本ブログシリーズはSAP Analytics CloudのBI機能を使ってダッシュボードやレポートの作成を行う方を対象とし、SAP Analytics CloudのBIにおける基本の操作やTipsをハンズオン形式でご紹介します。

本ブログでは、営業利益率や累計売上高など元々データとして入っていなかった値を計算させて表示される方法についてご紹介します。

 


 目次：

  • SAP Analytics Cloudにおける計算の追加
  • ストーリーでの計算式追加（営業利益率、累計売上高の計算）［ハンズオン］

※ ベースとなるサンプルストーリーの作成手順は、はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - サンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成 - SAP Communityでご紹介しています。

※ SAP Analytics Cloudは、四半期に１度のペースで製品のバージョンアップが実施されます。そのため今後のバージョンアップにより、本ブログで紹介する画面キャプチャや、操作が異なる可能性があることをご了承ください。（本ブログは2026年3月に作成しています。）

SAP Analytics Cloudにおける計算の追加

SAP Anaytics Cloudで計算式を追加する方法は、大きく以下の2つがあります。

  • モデルでの計算式追加
    計算式の定義場所をモデルで一元管理するため、複数のストーリーでモデルを利用した場合でも数字の整合性がとれ、統制のとれた計算の定義が可能
  • ストーリーでの計算式追加
    ストーリー作成時に新しい項目を任意に追加し、レポート上で利用することが可能であり、分析要件に柔軟に対応可能

今回は後者のストーリーでの計算式追加についてご紹介していきます。
ストーリーの編集モードで作成している際に、チャートに対して計算式を定義して新しい項目を追加することができます。計算には四則演算の演算子だけでなく、IF()やABS()などの関数や、ANDや>などの条件も使用することができます。さらには、平均値や累計値、差異などを定義するための設定もご用意しています。

詳細はヘルプページをご覧ください：チャートでのカスタム計算の作成 | SAP Help Portal

 

ストーリーでの計算式追加（営業利益率、累計売上高の計算）［ハンズオン］

0. はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - サンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成で作成したストーリーを編集モードで開こう！

0.1. ナビゲーションバーのファイルより作成したストーリーを開き、「編集」をクリックします。

0.2. 「Page_1」の右横の「v」をクリックし、「複製」をクリックしてページを複製します。

 

0.3. 「Page_1 (1)」の右横の「v」をクリックし、「タイトルの編集」をクリックし、タイトルを「計算」に変更します。

 

1. 営業利益率を計算させて表示しよう！

1.1. 数値ポイントチャートをクリックした上、右パネル（ビルダパネル）を開きます。

1.2. メジャー > 一次値 > 「＋メジャーの追加」をクリックします。

1.3. 「＋計算の追加」をクリックします。

1.4. 営業利益÷売上高の計算を追加します。タイプを「計算メジャー」を選択し、名前(ID)を「営業利益率」と入力します。式の編集欄で「[」と入力し、ポップアップで出てきたメジャーの候補から「営業利益」を選択します。

1.5. 続けて「/」を入力して、もう一度「[」と入力し、ポップアップで出てきたメジャーの候補から「売上高」を選択します。

1.6. 左下の「書式」をクリックし、「有効な式です。」と表示されることを確認します。「OK」をクリックします。

1.7. 書式を変更します。追加された営業利益率の「•••」をクリックし、「書式...」をクリックします。

1.8. 「基本のメジャーの単位を使用」のチェックを外してから 、スケールを「パーセント」に変更し、「OK」をクリックします。

1.9. 営業利益率が表示されました！

 

2. 累計売上高を計算させて表示させよう！

年度単位で累計値で計算させて、四半期ごとの累計を表示させてみよう！

 2.1. 売上高の棒チャートをクリックし、ビルダパネルにてメジャー > 売上高 > 「×」をクリックしてメジャーから外します。

2.2. メジャー > 「＋少なくとも1メジャーが必要です」をクリックします。

2.3. 「＋計算の追加」をクリックします。

2.4. 売上高を年度ごとに累計させる計算を作成します。タイプを「制限メジャー」と選択し、名前(ID)を「累計売上高」と入力します。メジャーを「売上高」、ディメンションを「月」、値を「ナビゲーション > 終了日付(累計) > 年度累計」を選択して「OK」をクリックします。
2.5. ディメンションが四半期ごとに設定されているため四半期ごとの累計が表示され、年度ごとに更新されていることが確認できます。

今回は計算の追加方法の紹介でした。SAP Analytics Cloudは他にも様々な表現や便利な機能があり、本ブログシリーズでご紹介しています。
はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - シリーズまとめ - SAP Communityからご参照ください。

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the Bengaluru SAP Community Group!!!
entry.content:
Hi, 

I am very pleased to be a part of the Bengaluru SAP community group. Looking forward to learn, grow and connect with the community participants.

 

Regards

Yashasvi Raina

entry.title: Upload rebate credit memo to KSEF (Polish National System of e-Invoices)
entry.content:
Dear All, I have question about passing rebate credit memos (bonus invoices) to KSEF from ECC.

Our company is using condition based rebate agreement scenario when we pay bonus to customers with quarterly rebate credit memo (via rebate settlement).

From document flow perspective credit memo request is a standalone one - without any reference to F2 invoices which accrued the rebate in the past quarter - so there is no direct link between original invoice and (bonus) credit memo.

If I understand well KSEF requirement is to display every reference invoices (or evenitems?) on the credit memo. But standard ECC Rebate Settlement process (when we credit 100+ invoices, 1000+ items in one shot) is not designed to support this.

Is there a SAP solution or workaround which makes rebate settlement process aligned with KSEF in ECC?

entry.title: Re: abapgit-agent: AI Coding Assistants for ABAP
entry.content: I’m glad to hear that.  I currently don’t have the required permissions on my client’s SAP system. Could you guide me through how you managed to do it? Do you have access to a test SAP environment? If so, how did you obtain it? Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Build Work Zone: Tile Opens(content federation) but App Fails with ‘IP Address Cannot Be Found
entry.content:
In my SAP Build Work Zone (Standard Edition) environment, I faced an issue where a dynamic tile loaded successfully through content federation, but clicking the tile caused the application to fail with a browser error:

ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED – The IP address of the server could not be found

The failing URL always contained a very long auto‑generated hostname.

 

Interestingly, the dynamic tile itself worked correctly (e.g., OData count displayed), but navigating to the corresponding SAP Fiori app consistently failed. This clearly indicated that the issue was not with backend connectivity or destinations, but with the content runtime URL generated by Work Zone.
 
Please refer the screenshots.

entry.title: Re: For outbound delivery types I want to keep transporta...
entry.content:
Your STO outbound deliveries are being blocked because they are now flagged as transportation planning relevant before EWM can act. This behavior was likely introduced by a customizing change or a support package in February 2026. The fix is to adjust the delivery type / transportation relevance settings or use a BAdI so that EWM can continue independently.

So the Recommended approach is

1) If TM is not required for STO flows:
Mark STO deliveries as “Not relevant for transportation planning” in delivery type customizing. This bypasses TM and lets EWM process independently.

2) If TM is required for some STOs but not all:
Use a BAdI (e.g., BADI_SD_SHIPMENTS) to control transportation relevance dynamically. For STO deliveries, skip TM distribution.

3) If TM is required but you want EWM parallel processing:
Adjust the integration profile so that EWM activities (picking, packing, staging) can proceed even if TM planning is pending.

entry.title: Re: Testing Cloud Integration Groovy 4
entry.content: Thanks Manish!

entry.title: Do you know what it takes to keep Fiori running smoothly?
entry.content:
A brand‑new Learning Journey has just landed on SAP Learning—and it’s one you won’t want to miss.
The SAP Fiori – System Administration learning journey is now live, offering a complete, structured path for everyone who wants to configure, operate, and optimize SAP Fiori landscapes like a pro.

This new Learning Journey provides:

  • Clear, structured guidance from basics to deep‑dive expertise
  • Practical, hands‑on configuration topics covering Gateway, Web Dispatcher, and app activation
  • End‑to‑end administration skills, including monitoring and troubleshooting
  • Coverage aligned with SAP’s evolving best practices for Fiori system administration

Who should explore It?

  • System administrators
  • Technical consultants
  • Anyone supporting SAP S/4HANA and Fiori landscapes
  • Learners aiming for the SAP Certified Technology Associate – SAP Fiori System Administration certification 

Explore the new learning journey here: 👉 here

entry.title: Re: Why can CHARG be filled in MSEG but remain empty in R...
entry.content:
1. Under which conditions is CHARG updated in RESB?
In RESB, the batch field (CHARG) is only filled if the reservation itself is created with a specific batch requirement (e.g., batch entered manually in the reservation or derived from batch-specific requirements like batch-specific stock determination). If the reservation is created without specifying a batch, RESB will remain blank. Standard reservations (e.g., from production orders) usually do not carry batch numbers unless explicitly required.

2. Why can CHARG be filled in MSEG but remain empty in RESB?
MSEG reflects the actual goods movement (e.g., goods issue, transfer posting). At this point, batch determination has already taken place, and the system must record the actual batch consumed. RESB is only a planned requirement. It does not get updated retroactively with the batch used during goods issue. That’s why you see CHARG in MSEG but not in RESB.

3. Is this standard SAP behavior?
Yes, this is standard SAP behavior. RESB is not intended to reflect the actual batch consumed; it only shows the requirement. The actual consumption is always documented in MSEG.

4. Does batch determination during goods issue update only MSEG and not RESB?
Correct. Batch determination during goods issue updates MSEG (and accounting documents if relevant). RESB remains unchanged because it represents the original reservation requirement, not the execution result.

5. Standard Logic Behind This Behavior

  • RESB = Planned requirement (reservation item). Batch is optional and only filled if explicitly required at reservation creation.
    MSEG = Actual posting (material document). Batch is mandatory if the material is batch-managed, and the system records the actual batch consumed.

SAP deliberately separates requirement planning (RESB) from execution posting (MSEG). This ensures that reservations remain stable and do not get retroactively altered by execution details.

entry.title: Re: abapgit-agent: AI Coding Assistants for ABAP
entry.content:
You need to have an SAP system where you can install and use abapGit, create ICF node, create code. Otherwise you won't be able to use abapgit-agent.  I do have an SAP system where I have authorization. 

You need to ask your client to get the needed authorization.

entry.title: Technical Issue: Authentication Popup in SAP Build Work Zone (Destination: abap-cloud-default)
entry.content:
I am currently encountering a persistent "Sign in" authentication prompt while accessing the "Simple App" within the SAP Build Work Zone (Trial environment).

Despite having the destination abap-cloud-default_abap-trial-fae783bdtrial-dev configured with OAuth2UserTokenExchange, the application is requesting manual credentials instead of passing the SSO token.

Technical Details:

  • Destination Name: abap-cloud-default_abap-trial-fae783bdtrial-dev

  • Authentication Type: OAuth2UserTokenExchange

  • Error Manifestation: Pop-up requesting Username/Password for the ABAP instance URL.

 Has anyone encountered this or found a solution? I would appreciate any insights. Thank you in advance!








_schema-version: "3.2"
ID: sample
description: Generated by Fiori Tools
version: 0.0.1
modules:
- name: sample-destination-content
  type: com.sap.application.content
  requires:
  - name: sample-destination-service
    parameters:
      content-target: true
  - name: sample-repo-host
    parameters:
      service-key:
        name: sample-repo-host-key
  - name: sample-uaa
    parameters:
      service-key:
        name: sample-uaa-key
  parameters:
    content:
      instance:
        destinations:
        - Name: sample_html_repo_host
          ServiceInstanceName: sample-html5-service
          ServiceKeyName: sample-repo-host-key
          sap.cloud.service: sample
        - Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange
          Name: sample_uaa
          ServiceInstanceName: sample-xsuaa-service
          ServiceKeyName: sample-uaa-key
          sap.cloud.service: sample
        existing_destinations_policy: update
  build-parameters:
    no-source: true
- name: sample-app-content
  type: com.sap.application.content
  path: .
  requires:
  - name: sample-repo-host
    parameters:
      content-target: true
  build-parameters:
    build-result: resources
    requires:
    - artifacts:
      - sample.zip
      name: sample
      target-path: resources/
- name: sample
  type: html5
  path: .
  build-parameters:
    build-result: dist
    builder: custom
    commands:
    - npm install
    - npm run build:cf
    supported-platforms: []
resources:
- name: sample-destination-service
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    config:
      HTML5Runtime_enabled: true
      init_data:
        instance:
          destinations:
          - Authentication: NoAuthentication
            Name: ui5
            ProxyType: Internet
            Type: HTTP
            URL: https://ui5.sap.com
          existing_destinations_policy: update
      version: 1.0.0
    service: destination
    service-name: sample-destination-service
    service-plan: lite
- name: sample-uaa
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    path: ./xs-security.json
    service: xsuaa
    service-name: sample-xsuaa-service
    service-plan: application
- name: sample-repo-host
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: html5-apps-repo
    service-name: sample-html5-service
    service-plan: app-host
parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true

entry.title: Attributive inspection number of defects
entry.content:
Hi

Chetan lad

entry.title: Re: fixed quantity in production order
entry.content: one more query. I have i have fixed the quantity as 10. System creating order as fixed qty-10, Now my senario is given below . When demand is 54 then system created fixed order 5 nos with qty 10 , how the balance 4 can be planned .Kindly pour some idea on this .Thank you

entry.title: Re: COPA Custom field creation through Fiori
entry.content:
Hi,

I suggest reading through the following articles:

  • sap note: 2453614 - FAQ: Universal Journal Extensibility & 3005736
  • this blog post

Essentially there are 3 types of way to create a COPA characteristic:

  1. From custom field app. With the latest updates in the Market Segment business context it is possible to use published cds views as data definition
  2. Classic approach. By creating a copa characteristic with TCODE KEA5 and then extending manually each cds view needed for reporting purposes. (not recommended)
  3. Hybrid approach. Creating a manual append in INCL_EEW_MARKET_SEGMENT_PS and then publishing the field with TCODE SCFD_EUI. This enables to extend reporting view directly from custom field app.

If COPA characteristics have already been created via KEA5, you may want to evaluate whether to continue with the classic approach or recreate them using newer methods. Keep in mind that with the second approach you must consider key and text values. I would reccommend reading this blog post.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Fixed Price Condition in Subcontracting PO – Prevent...
entry.content:
Why the System Prorates by Quantity
In SAP MM, pricing conditions (like PBXX) are inherently quantity-based. The system calculates the item value as price × GR quantity. At Goods Receipt, the system values the stock based on the received quantity and the condition value. That’s why if you receive 99 instead of 100, the GR value is automatically prorated.

Standard Behavior vs. Your Requirement

  • Standard SAP behavior: Always prorates condition values by GR quantity.
  • Your requirement: Keep the fixed total PO item value regardless of yield variance.
  • This is not achievable with a simple copy of PBXX, because the valuation logic at GR is hard-coded to be quantity-based.

Possible Approaches

1. Use Service Item for Manufacturing Fee

  • Model the subcontracting fee as a service line item (limit PO or service PO) with a fixed value.
  • The material line covers the components and finished goods.
  • The service line covers the fixed manufacturing fee.
  • At GR, the material valuation is based on components, while the vendor’s fee is settled via the service invoice.
  • This way, the fee is not prorated by yield.

2. Invoice-Based Settlement (Recommended)

  • Accept that GR will always be quantity-based.
  • Post the fixed vendor fee at invoice posting, not at GR.
  • The invoice can be entered for the full $100 regardless of GR quantity.
  • This avoids valuation inconsistencies and aligns with your note that invoices are often posted long after GR.

3. Custom Condition Type with Fixed Value

  • Technically possible, but requires custom development (BAdI in MM pricing or GR valuation).
  • You’d need to override the valuation logic so that the condition is applied as a fixed item value, not prorated.
  • This is not supported out-of-the-box and would be a deviation from SAP standard.

Accounting Treatment

  • Components: Issued to subcontractor at average price.
  • Finished goods: Received at GR, valued at component cost (plus any prorated conditions).
  • Vendor fee: Best practice is to post at invoice stage as a fixed amount.

This ensures that yield variances don’t distort GR valuation.

entry.title: Re: International Editable SALV Day 2026 – Year 18

...at least, that's a community stable, nice to still have that *thumbsup*

entry.title: Re: CPI should call a report (with Selection-Screen) in S...
entry.content:
@mrittiz 

Reports cannot be called from CPI. However, if the report uses a function module or a class for retrieving the data, you can call that API from a CDS custom entity which can be exposed as an OData service. Alternatively, if the data source is a DDIC table, you can wrap it with a CDS View and expose it as an OData service as well.

entry.title: Re: Can we call a remote API to update a business object in S4 using a custom entity in BTP ABAP?
entry.content:
@rammel 

Yes, I believe it is possible to call an OData service within the save_modified method to update the remote data. You can use unmanaged save for that scenario.

entry.title: Re: The Internal Party Flag in SAP Sales Cloud V2
entry.content:
Hi Ashwin

Can you tell how to enable same in Sales cloud V1.  I am trying to enable it is not enabling.  

Regards

Harish.A

Sap C4C Consultant

entry.title: Re: Can we call a remote API to update a business object in S4 using a custom entity in BTP ABAP?
entry.content: From my experience you can't do things like remote RFC calls or HTTP/REST/OData calls in the save phase.  You can however initiate a bgPF task and do the call there.

entry.title: Is Actual Costing (Scope Item 33Q) feasible in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud with a mix of Price Control?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

As part of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance  project, I have an important point regarding Actual Costing (Scope Item 33Q)

Is Actual Costing (Scope Item 33Q) feasible in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud with a mix of Price Control V and S?

Following an internal review of the valuation setup, we identified the following situation in the customer’s system:

  • Raw materials (RM) are valuated with Moving Average Price / Price Control V
  • Semi‑finished (SFG) and finished goods (FG) are valuated with Standard Price / Price Control S and Moving Average Price / Price Control V

From our understanding, Actual Costing in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud requires all materials to be valuated with Standard Price (S) in order to work properly.

Question:
* Can Actual Costing (Scope Item 33Q) still be activated in a scenario where materials are set to Price Control V and others to Price Control S? Or is it mandatory in Public Cloud to set all materials to Standard Price (S) to use Actual Costing?

* If Actual Costing cannot be used due to the presence of materials under Price Control V, are there any alternative solutions or processes available in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to calculate or approximate the actual cost

Thank you!

entry.content:

I am getting an error in vk11 which says 'Enter a valid Value'. Is there anything wrong with my material. 

Also, I am a fresher just trying to learn,  so plz send me a step wise solution. 

Thanks,

Meghana

entry.title: Kick-Start Your SAP Logistics Management Implementation with the New Customer Onboarding Journey
entry.content:
Are you a new SAP Logistics Management customer preparing to launch your implementation project?

To help you begin with confidence and set your project up for long-term success, we’ve created a dedicated Customer Onboarding Journey designed specifically for the SAP Logistics Management solution.

Your first stop is the SAP Logistics Management Onboarding Resource Center - your one-stop shop where you’ll find everything you need for a smooth and guided start. Explore a comprehensive collection of ungated enablement resources, including:

  • Step-by-step instructions for using SAP for Me
  • Guidance on system access
  • Helpful insights on what to expect during the project lifecycle
  • Best practices to prepare your team for the upcoming implementation

Your 4 Phase Onboarding Journey

All content in the SAP Logistics Management Onboarding Resource Center is organized into four clear phases, helping you progress seamlessly from contract signature to Go-Live.

 Phase 1. Get Started with SAP

This phase ensures you understand how you can access your SAP Logistics Management system and which communications to expect from SAP throughout the process.

You will also learn about the key SAP channels, especially SAP for Me, and how to receive support for both technical and non-technical questions.

Main topics include:

  1. Introduction to your onboarding journey
  2. SAP channels & S-user setup
  3. System access
  4. SAP Support essentials

Phase 2. Plan Your Implementation

This phase gives you a high-level understanding of the tools and resources available in your subscription and how to use them to plan, run, and accelerate your project implementation.

Main topics include:

  1. Running your project with SAP Activate
  2. Managing your project with SAP Cloud ALM
  3. Channels and offerings that support your implementation
  4. Roles, responsibilities & system testing

Phase 3. Get Enabled

Here, you’ll explore free learning and enablement resources to help your team build the skills required to work effectively with the SAP Logistics Management solution.

Main topics include:

  1. Key features of SAP Logistics Management
  2. Offerings and channels that deepen your solution knowledge
  3. Ways to connect with SAP experts and peers

Phase 4. Prepare to Go Live

The final phase focuses on ensuring a smooth Go-Live and preparing you to run your business successfully with SAP Logistics Management.

Main topics include:

  1. Final project preparation
  2. Post Go-Live support and available channels
  3. Guidance on continuous innovation

Key Takeaways

The SAP Logistics Management Customer Onboarding Journey is thoughtfully designed to guide you from day one, ensuring you’re fully prepared for a successful implementation - from contract signature all the way to Go-Live.

Take time to explore the available resources, plan your project, and engage with the tools and support channels provided.

Your path to a successful SAP Logistics Management Go-Live starts here.

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps for SAP Launchpad Service – Part 2
entry.content:
Hello @matthias_schmalz,

1. Fiori App1 uses reuse lib
2. Reuse lib uses its own OData service

When I deployed to BTP CF apps, 
1. Fiori App1 own OData requests goes with guid+appid/sap/opu/odata/.... and it is working fine
within this resuse lib accessing its own OData without guid+appid  /sap/opu/odata/.... 404 not found

Please let me know how to fix it?

entry.title: Support Proficiency: Recognizing Partners with Trusted Support Expertise
entry.content:
SAP cloud customers rely on implementation and operations services from SAP’s partner ecosystem, where an effective support collaboration with SAP is essential for customer success.

Partners that have built strong capabilities for an effective support collaboration can accelerate project execution and reduce risks. For example, by self-sufficiently addressing customers’ consulting and “how-to” questions, or by leveraging SAP’s best practices and recommendations.

Customers are therefore encouraged to review partners’ effectiveness in support collaboration when selecting and managing partners. To simplify partner selection, SAP has introduced a new support proficiency designation in SAP Partner Finder, which empowers customers to choose partners with trusted support expertise. Consistent with the Competency Framework regulations, the new designation expands existing partner recognitions. It distinguishes partners with established capabilities for minimizing delays and preventing project risks as well as with an effective support collaboration with SAP.

Details available on the SAP Support Portal and SAP Partner Portal (partners only)

entry.title: Re: eDocument Hungary for Invoice cancellation
entry.content:
Hello Jonas,

We implemented S4 Public Cloud recently and have encountered the same problem as you. Have you found a solution? What is troubling is that sending the cancellation is mandatory in Hungary and that apparently SAP doesn't support this. Any help would be appreciated.

Thanks!
